message:
perf(transport): Deferred buffer compaction in codec

Replace immediate drain() after each decode with deferred compaction.
Tracks read position and only compacts when it exceeds 4KB threshold.

Benefits:
- Most decode calls are now O(1) pointer updates instead of O(n) memmove
- Reduces memory churn for high-throughput streaming protocols
- Amortizes compaction cost over many small messages

Trade-off: Up to 4KB of consumed bytes may remain in buffer before cleanup.

Isomorphism: Output identical - same messages in same order.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
